records.chapter: Wealth transforms exile into homeland while poverty estranges homeland
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 56. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: With wealth a strange land is a homeland, while with destitution even a homeland is a strange land. [1]
records.arabicText: ٥٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْغِنَى فِي الْغُرْبَةِ وَطَنٌ، وَالْفَقْرُ فِي الْوَطَنِ غُرْبَةٌ.

records.chapter: Warning as a form of good tidings
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 59. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever warns you is like one who gives you good tidings.
records.arabicText: ٥٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ حَذَّرَكَ كَمَنْ بَشَّرَكَ.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 417. Someone said before Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him: “astaghfiru’llāh” (I ask Allāh’s forgiveness), then Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said:
records.arabicText: ٤١٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِقَائِلٍ قَالَ بِحَضْرَتِهِ: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Your mother may lose you! Do you know what “istighfār” (asking Allāh’s forgiveness) is? “istighfār” is meant for people of a high position. It is a word that stands on six supports.
records.arabicText: ثَكِلَتْكَ أُمُّكَ، أَتَدْرِي مَا الِاسْتِغْفَارُ؟ (إنَّ) الِاسْتِغْفَارُ دَرَجَةُ الْعِلِّيِّينَ، وَهُوَ اسْمٌ وَاقِعٌ عَلَى سِتَّةِ مَعَانٍ:

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: The first is to repent over the past;
records.arabicText: أَوَّلُهَا: النَّدَمُ عَلَى مَا مَضَى.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: the second is to make a firm determination never to revert to it;
records.arabicText: وَالثَّانِي: الْعَزْمُ عَلَى تَرْكِ الْعَوْدِ إِلَيْهِ أَبَداً.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: the third is to discharge all the rights of people so that you may meet Allāh quite clean with nothing to account for;
records.arabicText: وَالثَّالِثُ: أَنْ تُؤَدِّيَ إِلَى الْـمَخْلُوقِينَ حُقُوقَهُمْ حَتَّى تَلْقَى اللهَ عزّوجلّ أَمْلَسَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ تَبِعَةٌ.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: the fourth is to fulfil every obligation which you ignored (in the past) so that you may now do justice with it;
records.arabicText: وَالرَّابِعُ: أَنْ تَعْمِدَ إِلَى كُلِّ فَرِيضَةٍ عَلَيْكَ ضَيَّعْتَهَا فَتُؤَدِّيَ حَقَّهَا.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: the fifth is to aim at the flesh grown as a result of unlawful earning, so that you may melt it by grief (of repentance) till the skin touches the bone and a new flesh grows between them;
records.arabicText: وَالْخَامِسُ: أَنْ تَعْمِدَ إِلَى اللَّحْمِ الَّذِي نَبَتَ عَلَى السُّحْتِ فَتُذِيبَهُ بِالْأَحْزَانِ، حَتَّى يَلْصِقَ الْجِلْدُ بِالْعَظْمِ، وَيَنْشَأَ بَيْنَهُمَا لَحْمٌ جَدِيدٌ.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: and the sixth is to make the body taste the pain of obedience as you (previously) made it taste the sweetness of disobedience.
records.arabicText: وَالسَّادِسُ: أَنْ تُذِيقَ الْجِسْمَ أَلَمَ الطَّاعَةِ كَمَا أَذَقْتَهُ حَلاَوَةَ الْمَعْصِيَةِ.

records.chapter: True meaning of istighfār in Islam
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: On such an occasion you may say “astaghfiru’llāh”.
records.arabicText: فَعِنْدَ ذلِكَ تَقُولُ: أَسْتَغْفِرُاللهَ.

records.chapter: Human frailty and mortality
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 419. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: How wretched the son of Adam (man) is! His death is hidden, his ailments are concealed, his actions are preserved, the bite of a mosquito pains him, choking causes his death and sweat gives him a bad smell.
records.arabicText: ٤١٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مِسْكِينٌ ابْنُ آدَمَ: مَكْتُومُ الْأَجَلِ، مَكْنُونُ الْعِلَلِ، مَحْفُوظُ الْعَمَلِ، تَؤْلِمُهُ الْبَقَّةُ، وَتَقْتُلُهُ الشَّرْقَةُ وَتُنْتِنُهُ الْعَرْقَةُ.

records.chapter: Ali's counsel on restraining lustful gazes
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 420. It is related that Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, was sitting with his companions when a beautiful woman passed by them and they began to look at her whereupon Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said:
records.arabicText: ٤٢٠. وَرُوِيَ أَنَّهُ (عليه السلام) كَانَ جَالِساً فِي أَصْحَابِهِ، فَمَرَّتْ بِهِمُ امْرَأَةٌ جَمِيلَةٌ، فَرَمَقَهَا الْقَوْمُ بِأَبْصَارِهِمْ فَقَالَ (عليه السلام) :

records.chapter: Ali's counsel on restraining lustful gazes
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: The eyes of these men are covetous and this glancing is the cause of their becoming covetous. Whenever anyone of you sees a woman who attracts him, he should meet his wife because she is a woman like his wife.
records.arabicText: إِنَّ أَبْصَار هذِهِ الْفُحُولِ طَوَامِحُ وَإِنَّ ذلِكَ سَبَبُ هَبَابِهَا فإِذَا نَظَرَ أَحَدُكُمْ إِلَى امْرَأَةٍ تُعْجِبُهُ فَلْيُلاَمِسْ أَهْلَهُ، فَإِنَّمَا هِيَ امْرَأَهٌ كَامْرَأَةٍ.

records.chapter: Ali's counsel on restraining lustful gazes
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Then, one of the Khārijites said: Allāh may kill this heretic. How logical he is! The people then leapt towards him to kill him but Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said:
records.arabicText: فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْخَوَارِجِ: قَاتَلَهُ اللَّهُ، كَافِراً مَا أَفْقَهَهُ. فَوَثَبَ الْقَوْمُ لِيَقْتُلُوهُ، فَقَالَ (عليه السلام) :

records.chapter: Ali's counsel on restraining lustful gazes
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Wait a bit. There should either be abuse for abuse or else pardon from the offence.
records.arabicText: رُوَيْداً إِنَّمَا هُوَ سَبٌّ بِسَبٍّ، أَوْ عَفْوٌ عَنْ ذَنْبٍ.

records.chapter: "Do good regardless of its perceived size"
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 422. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Do good and do not regard any part of it small because its small is big and its little is much. No one of you should say that another person is more deserving than I in doing good. Otherwise, by Allāh, it would really be so. There are people of good and evil. When you would leave either of the two, others will perform them.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): افْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ وَلَا تَحْقِرُوا مِنْهُ شَيْئاً، فَإِنَّ صَغِيرَهُ كَبِيرٌ وَقَلِيلَهُ كَثِيرٌ، وَلَا يَقُولَنَّ أَحَدُكُمْ: إِنَّ أَحَداً أَوْلَى بِفِعْلِ الْخَيْرِ مِنِّي فَيَكُونَ وَاللهِ كَذلِكَ، إِنَّ لِلْخَيْرِ وَالشَّرِّ أَهْلاً، فَمَهْمَا تَرَكْتُمُوهُ مِنْهُمَا كَفَاكُمُوهُ أَهْلُهُ.

records.chapter: Inner righteousness ensures divine favor in worldly affairs
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 423. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever set right his inward self, Allāh sets right his outward self. Whoever performs acts for his religion, Allāh accomplishes his acts of this world. Whoever’s dealings between himself and Allāh are good, Allāh turns the dealings between him and other people good.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ أَصْلَحَ سَرِيرَتَهُ أَصْلَحَ اللهُ عَلاَنِيَتَهُ، وَمَنْ عَمِلَ لِدِينِهِ كَفَاهُ اللهُ أَمْرَ دُنْيَاهُ، وَمَنْ أَحْسَنَ فِيَما بَيْنَهُ وبَيْنَ اللهِ، أَحْسَنَ اللَّهُ مَا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ النَّاسِ.

records.chapter: Divine favors granted through generosity
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 425. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: There are some creatures of Allāh whom Allāh particularizes with favours for the benefit of the people, therefore He stays them in their hands so long as they give them to others; but when they deny them to others He takes away the favours from them and sends them to others.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ لِلَّهِ عِبَاداً يَخْتَصُّهُمُ اللَّهُ بِالنِّعَمِ لِمَنَافِعِ الْعِبَادِ، فَيُقِرُّهَا فِي أَيْدِيهِمْ مَا بَذَلُوهَا، فَإِذَا مَنَعُوهَا نَزَعَهَا مِنْهُمْ، ثُمَّ حَوَّلَهَا إِلَى غَيْرِهِمْ.

records.chapter: Transience of health and wealth
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 426. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon them, said: It does not behove a man to have trust in two positions, health and riches, because there is many a man whom you see healthy but he soon falls sick and many a man whom you see rich but soon turns destitute.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْعَبْدِ أَنْ يَثِقَ بِخَصْلَتَيْنِ: الْعَافِيَةِ، وَالْغِنَى: بَيْنَا تَرَاهُ مُعَافًى إِذْ سَقِمَ، وَبَيْنَا تَرَاهُ غَنِيّاً إِذِ افْتَقَرَ.

records.chapter: True celebration in divine acceptance and sinlessness
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 428. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said on the occcasion of an ‘īd (Muslim feast day): It is an ‘īd for him whose fasting Allāh accepts and for whose prayers He is grateful; and (in fact) every day wherein no sin of Allāh is committed is an ‘īd. [1]
records.arabicText: ٤٢٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) فِی بَعْضِ الْأَعْيَادِ: إِنَّمَا هُوَ عِيدٌ لِمَنْ قَبلَ اللهُ صِيَامَهُ وَشَكَرَ قِيَامَهُ، وَكُلُّ يَوْمٍ لَا يُعْصَى اللهُ فِيهِ فَهُوَ (يَوْمَ) عِيدٌ.

records.chapter: Regret of sinful wealth in the Hereafter
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 429. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: On the Day of Judgement the greatest regret will be felt by the man who earned wealth through sinful ways, although it is inherited by a person who spends it in obeying Allāh, the Glorified, and he will be awarded Paradise on that account while the first one will go into the Fire on account of it.
records.arabicText: ٤٢٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ أَعْظَمَ الْحَسَرَاتِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ حَسْرَةُ رَجُلٍ كَسَبَ مَالًا فِي غَيْرِ طَاعَةِ اللهِ، فَوَرَّثَهُ رَجُلٌ فَأنْفَقَهُ فِي طَاعَةِ اللهِ سُبْحَانَهُ، فَدَخَلَ بِهِ الْجَنَّةَ، وَدَخَلَ الْأَوَّلُ بِهِ النَّارَ.

records.chapter: Two types of livelihood: worldly pursuit and spiritual seeking
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 431. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Livelihood is of two kinds: the seeker and the sought. Therefore, he who hankers after this world death traces him till it turns him out of it; but he who hankers after the next world, worldly ease itself seeks him till he receives his livelihood from it.
records.arabicText: ٤٣١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الرِّزْقُ رِزْقَانِ: طَالِبٌ، وَمَطْلُوبٌ، فَمَنْ طَلَبَ الدُّنْيَا طَلَبَهُ الْمَوْتُ حَتَّى يُخْرِجَهُ عَنْهَا، وَمَنْ طَلَبَ الْآخِرَةَ طَلَبَتْهُ الدُّنْيَا حَتَّى يَسْتَوْفِيَ رِزْقَهُ مِنْهَا.

records.chapter: Transient pleasures and lasting consequences
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 433. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Remember that pleasures will pass away while the consequences will stay
records.arabicText: ٤٣٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): اذْكُرُوا انْقِطَاعَ الَّلذَّاتِ، وَبَقَاءَ التَّبِعَاتِ.

records.chapter: Testing reveals a man's flaws
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 434. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Try (a man) and you will hate him.
records.arabicText: ٤٣٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): اخْبُرْ تَقْلِهِ.
